concepts.title: Definition of Partnership
concepts.description: A partnership is an association of two or more persons who have agreed to share profits and losses of a business carried on by them together.

concepts.title: Key Characteristics of Partnership
concepts.description: A partnership is formed on the basis of a partnership deed and is characterised by mutual agency, mutual profits and losses, and unlimited liability of partners.

concepts.title: Types of Partnerships
concepts.description: There are three types of partnerships - General Partnership, Limited Partnership, and Limited Liability Partnership (LLP).

concepts.title: Hierarchy of Partnership
concepts.description: To remember the hierarchy of partnership - 'Majority Decides, Minority Adheres, Minority Adheres to Majority' - Majority Partner's interest is first considered, then Minority Partner's interest is determined.

concepts.title: Difference between Agency and Partnership
concepts.description: Agency refers to a relationship between a principal and an agent, whereas partnership is a relationship between two or more persons carrying on business together. Partnership is not a type of agency.

concepts.title: Rights and Liabilities of Partners
concepts.description: Partners have equal rights and liabilities in a partnership firm, unless otherwise specified in the partnership deed. Partners are jointly and severally liable for the firm's debts and obligations.

concepts.category: One of the most important formulae to remember
concepts.title: Profit Sharing Ratio Formula
concepts.description: The profit sharing ratio is calculated based on the ratio of the initial investment of each partner multiplied by the duration of the partnership.

concepts.title: Advantages of Partnership
concepts.description: Partnership provides advantages such as shared risk, shared expertise, and flexibility in decision-making.
